steps.title: Identify the chronological signal
steps.content: The student's explicit goal is to “emphasize the order in which the two films were released”—showing which film came first and which came second.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A uses temporal language (“early in her career,” “years later”) to frame the two dates as distinct points in a timeline, directly emphasizing their chronological relationship.

The other choices either focus on her changing roles (B), present both films as examples without highlighting sequence (C), or mention only one film (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
